pattern: A counter for the untouched tail of an infinite sequence plus a min-heap (with a membership guard) for re-added values gives O(log n) pops without materializing infinitely many numbers.
hints: You can never store every positive integer, but everything above some threshold is still present and contiguous, so a single boundary number describes that whole tail.
Numbers that are added back are exactly the ones below the boundary that you previously popped, so keep them separately and always prefer the smallest of them.
Track the next never-popped integer with a counter, and keep added-back values in a min-heap plus a set to avoid duplicates; popSmallest returns the smaller of the heap top and the counter.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: addBack of a present number is a no-op, then a popped number added back reappears first
pop several from the fresh set with no addBacks
addBack before any pop never changes the answer since everything is already present
duplicate addBack of the same popped value must not let it be popped twice
a re-added small value outranks the running counter on the next pop
addBack of a number that was never popped (above current usage but smaller than counter would matter) stays absent if already present
exhaust into the high range then add back a low number

stress.description: long randomized mix of pops and addBacks over a small value space so re-adds collide
stress.note: tracks previously-popped values to feed realistic addBack arguments
